affected_pattern: Active customer with InternetService=Fiber, MonthlyCharges suddenly 0
root_cause: Billing system failed to process CDR for current cycle. Likely cause: CDR ingestion pipeline timeout during batch processing window, resulting in zero-rated billing record.
supporting_evidence: 3GPP TS 32.240 Section 5.2 — CDR processing timeout handling
SLA Document — Section 3.1: Billing accuracy guarantee >= 99.5%
Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: CDR Ingestion Pipeline Timeout pattern
recommended_action: Trigger CDR reprocessing for affected account
Verify CDR pipeline health for batch window
Issue corrective billing statement

affected_pattern: Customer with active PhoneService and multiple streaming services showing $0 monthly charge
root_cause: Provisioning system mismatch — customer was provisioned for services but billing system not updated. Service activation event did not propagate to billing platform.
supporting_evidence: Provisioning-to-billing sync protocol documentation
Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Provisioning System Mismatch pattern
CRM shows active services but billing system shows inactive
recommended_action: Resync provisioning system with billing platform
Issue corrective bill for missed billing cycles
Audit provisioning event queue for other stuck messages

affected_pattern: Multiple customers on same tariff plan showing zero charges in same billing cycle
root_cause: Rating engine configuration error — incorrect tariff plan configuration rating service usage at $0 for the specific plan variant.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Rating Engine Configuration Error pattern
Multiple customers on same plan affected simultaneously
Recent configuration change to tariff plan identified in change log
recommended_action: Correct tariff configuration in rating engine
Reprocess all affected CDRs for the billing cycle
Implement configuration change validation checks
estimated_revenue_impact: Sum of expected charges for all affected customers

affected_pattern: Customer billed twice for same monthly charges with identical timestamps
root_cause: CDR deduplication engine failure — the deduplication process was bypassed during a system restart, allowing duplicate CDR records to pass through to the rating engine.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: CDR Deduplication Engine Failure pattern
3GPP TS 32.240 Section 4.3 — CDR uniqueness requirements
System restart event logged 30 minutes before duplicate CDRs appeared
recommended_action: Remove duplicate CDRs from billing records
Issue refund/credit for overcharged amount
Restart and validate deduplication engine
Reprocess affected billing records
estimated_revenue_impact: Duplicate charge amount per affected customer

affected_pattern: Customer shows doubled MonthlyCharges with two billing entries separated by seconds
root_cause: Billing system retry logic error — the retry mechanism for a failed transaction duplicated the charge when the original transaction actually succeeded but confirmation was lost.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Billing System Retry Logic Error pattern
Two charge entries with same amount but different transaction IDs
System timeout event logged between the two transactions
recommended_action: Implement transaction-level idempotency checks
Reverse the duplicate charge
Add unique transaction ID verification before charge posting
Audit retry logic configuration

affected_pattern: Enterprise customer with bundled services billed by two different billing subsystems
root_cause: Cross-system synchronization failure — voice and data billing systems both processed the same usage events for a bundled service plan, resulting in double billing.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Cross-System Synchronization Failure pattern
Charges from two different billing system IDs for same usage
Recent change to billing system orchestration routing rules
recommended_action: Fix routing rules to prevent cross-system duplicate processing
Reconcile and reverse duplicate charges
Strengthen inter-system deduplication checks
estimated_revenue_impact: Total duplicate amount across affected accounts

affected_pattern: Customer data usage increased 10x overnight with charges far above historical baseline
root_cause: Potential fraudulent SIM cloning or account compromise — unauthorized usage detected from geographic locations inconsistent with customer's profile.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Fraudulent SIM Cloning pattern
Usage from two different geographic locations simultaneously
Customer profile shows no travel history
recommended_action: Immediately suspend account for security investigation
Issue replacement SIM to customer
Reverse fraudulent charges
File fraud report with security team

affected_pattern: Monthly charges jumped to 10x normal but usage pattern shows continuous 24/7 data consumption
root_cause: Device malware or malfunctioning application generating excessive background data usage. Customer's device has a rogue process consuming data continuously.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Device Malware pattern
Continuous 24/7 usage pattern (not human-like burst pattern)
Data usage is upload-heavy (malware exfiltration indicator)
recommended_action: Advise customer on device security scan
Apply retroactive data cap if plan allows
Consider goodwill credit for device-related issue
Flag for customer outreach

affected_pattern: Multiple customers on same plan showing exactly 10x usage spike simultaneously
root_cause: Rating engine metering error — a bug in the rating engine multiplied usage quantities by an incorrect factor (10x) for all customers on the affected plan.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Metering/Rating Error pattern
Multiple customers on same plan affected simultaneously
Spike is exactly 10x normal (round multiplier)
Recent rating engine configuration change identified
recommended_action: Correct rating engine calculation logic
Reprocess all affected CDRs with correct metering
Issue corrective billing for all affected customers
Implement rating accuracy regression tests
estimated_revenue_impact: Overcharged amount across all affected customers

affected_pattern: Customer billing record has NULL TotalCharges indicating CDR processing failure
root_cause: CDR format mismatch after network element upgrade — the CDR format changed but the mediation system was not updated, causing field parsing failures and NULL values in billing records.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Format Mismatch After Network Upgrade pattern
Network upgrade event logged 24 hours before failures started
CDR rejection logs show format-specific parsing errors
recommended_action: Update mediation device CDR parsing rules for new format
Reprocess all rejected CDRs since the network upgrade
Validate end-to-end CDR processing after fix
Add format validation to pre-upgrade checklist
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage for all affected accounts

affected_pattern: Batch of customer records with missing TotalCharges during peak traffic period
root_cause: Data pipeline overflow during peak traffic — CDR processing pipeline buffers overflowed during peak hours, causing CDRs to be dropped and resulting in NULL billing fields.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Data Pipeline Overflow pattern
Failures correlate with peak traffic hours (18:00-22:00)
Pipeline throughput metrics show capacity exceeded 120%
recommended_action: Increase CDR pipeline buffer capacity
Implement backpressure mechanisms
Scale mediation infrastructure for peak traffic
Replay lost CDRs from network element raw files
estimated_revenue_impact: Lost CDR value during overflow period

affected_pattern: Scattered NULL fields across multiple customer records with no clear time pattern
root_cause: Database storage failure — the CDR storage database experienced intermittent connection pool exhaustion, preventing random CDR records from being fully persisted.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Database Storage Failure pattern
Database connection pool exhaustion errors in system logs
Random pattern of failures (not correlated with time or customer segment)
recommended_action: Expand database connection pool size
Implement connection pool monitoring and alerting
Replay lost CDRs from mediation device buffer
Verify database health and storage capacity
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage for accounts with NULL records

affected_pattern: Enterprise customer monthly charges exceed contract cap by 50-200%
root_cause: Contract threshold not applied in rating engine — the customer's billing cap was not properly configured, allowing charges to exceed the contractual maximum.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Contract Threshold Not Applied pattern
CRM shows billing cap of $500/month but current charge is $1200
Rating engine configuration audit shows missing cap rule for this account
recommended_action: Correct rating engine configuration with proper billing cap
Reprocess bills from the date cap should have been applied
Issue refund for all overcharges above contract cap
Calculate and apply SLA penalty credits per contract terms
estimated_revenue_impact: Refund amount = (actual charges - cap) x affected_months

affected_pattern: Customer charges spiked after plan migration with charges at default uncapped rates
root_cause: Plan migration error — during plan upgrade, old plan billing rules were deactivated before new plan rules were fully configured, resulting in default (uncapped) billing rates.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Plan Migration Error pattern
Plan change event logged 5 days before charges spiked
Billing applied default/fallback rates instead of new plan rates
recommended_action: Apply correct plan rules retroactively from migration date
Refund excess charges above new plan rates
Fix plan migration workflow to prevent future gaps
Audit all recent plan migrations for similar issues
estimated_revenue_impact: Excess charges from migration date to detection

affected_pattern: Charges exceed plan limits due to previously included usage now classified as premium
root_cause: Usage type reclassification — a change in usage classification rules caused previously included usage to be reclassified as out-of-plan premium usage, incurring additional charges.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Usage Type Reclassification pattern
Classification rule change logged 2 weeks before SLA breach
Multiple customers on similar plans affected
recommended_action: Revert incorrect usage classification
Reprocess bills for all affected customers
Issue credits for incorrectly classified usage charges
Implement classification change impact analysis before deployment
estimated_revenue_impact: Total incorrect premium charges across affected customers
